org.apache.flink.connector.file.src

## Class FileSource<T>

  * [java.lang.Object](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Object.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")
  *     * [org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.AbstractFileSource](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/AbstractFileSource.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src")<T,[FileSourceSplit](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSourceSplit.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src")>
    *       * org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.FileSource<T>

  * Type Parameters:
    `T` \- The type of the events/records produced by this source.

All Implemented Interfaces:
    [Serializable](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/io/Serializable.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.io"), [Source](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/source/Source.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.source")<T,[FileSourceSplit](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSourceSplit.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src"),[PendingSplitsCheckpoint](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/PendingSplitsCheckpoint.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src")<[FileSourceSplit](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSourceSplit.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src")>>, [ResultTypeQueryable](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/typeutils/ResultTypeQueryable.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.java.typeutils")<T>

* * *




        [@PublicEvolving](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/annotation/PublicEvolving.html> "annotation in org.apache.flink.annotation")
        public final class FileSource<T>
        extends [AbstractFileSource](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/AbstractFileSource.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src")<T,[FileSourceSplit](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSourceSplit.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src")>

A unified data source that reads files - both in batch and in streaming mode.

This source supports all (distributed) file systems and object stores that can be accessed via the Flink's [`FileSystem`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/core/fs/FileSystem.html> "class in org.apache.flink.core.fs") class.

Start building a file source via one of the following calls:

    * [`forRecordStreamFormat(StreamFormat, Path...)`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSource.html#forRecordStreamFormat-org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.reader.StreamFormat-org.apache.flink.core.fs.Path...->)
    * [`forBulkFileFormat(BulkFormat, Path...)`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSource.html#forBulkFileFormat-org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.reader.BulkFormat-org.apache.flink.core.fs.Path...->)
    * [`forRecordFileFormat(FileRecordFormat, Path...)`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSource.html#forRecordFileFormat-org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.reader.FileRecordFormat-org.apache.flink.core.fs.Path...->)

This creates a [`FileSource.FileSourceBuilder`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSource.FileSourceBuilder.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src") on which you can configure all the properties of the file source.

## Batch and Streaming

This source supports both bounded/batch and continuous/streaming data inputs. For the bounded/batch case, the file source processes all files under the given path(s). In the continuous/streaming case, the source periodically checks the paths for new files and will start reading those.

When you start creating a file source (via the [`FileSource.FileSourceBuilder`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSource.FileSourceBuilder.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src") created through one of the above-mentioned methods) the source is by default in bounded/batch mode. Call [`AbstractFileSource.AbstractFileSourceBuilder.monitorContinuously(Duration)`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/AbstractFileSource.AbstractFileSourceBuilder.html#monitorContinuously-java.time.Duration->) to put the source into continuous streaming mode.

## Format Types

The reading of each file happens through file readers defined by _file formats_. These define the parsing logic for the contents of the file. There are multiple classes that the source supports. Their interfaces trade of simplicity of implementation and flexibility/efficiency.

    * A [`StreamFormat`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/reader/StreamFormat.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.reader") reads the contents of a file from a file stream. It is the simplest format to implement, and provides many features out-of-the-box (like checkpointing logic) but is limited in the optimizations it can apply (such as object reuse, batching, etc.).
    * A [`BulkFormat`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/reader/BulkFormat.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.reader") reads batches of records from a file at a time. It is the most "low level" format to implement, but offers the greatest flexibility to optimize the implementation.
    * A [`FileRecordFormat`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/reader/FileRecordFormat.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.reader") is in the middle of the trade-off spectrum between the `StreamFormat` and the `BulkFormat`.

## Discovering / Enumerating Files

The way that the source lists the files to be processes is defined by the [`FileEnumerator`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/enumerate/FileEnumerator.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.enumerate"). The `FileEnumerator` is responsible to select the relevant files (for example filter out hidden files) and to optionally splits files into multiple regions (= file source splits) that can be read in parallel).

See Also:
    [Serialized Form](<../../../../../../serialized-form.html#org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.FileSource>)

  *     * ### Field Detail

      * #### DEFAULT_SPLIT_ASSIGNER

            public static final [FileSplitAssigner.Provider](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/assigners/FileSplitAssigner.Provider.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.assigners") DEFAULT_SPLIT_ASSIGNER

The default split assigner, a lazy locality-aware assigner.

      * #### DEFAULT_SPLITTABLE_FILE_ENUMERATOR

            public static final [FileEnumerator.Provider](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/enumerate/FileEnumerator.Provider.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.enumerate") DEFAULT_SPLITTABLE_FILE_ENUMERATOR

The default file enumerator used for splittable formats. The enumerator recursively enumerates files, split files that consist of multiple distributed storage blocks into multiple splits, and filters hidden files (files starting with '.' or '_'). Files with suffixes of common compression formats (for example '.gzip', '.bz2', '.xy', '.zip', ...) will not be split.

      * #### DEFAULT_NON_SPLITTABLE_FILE_ENUMERATOR

            public static final [FileEnumerator.Provider](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/enumerate/FileEnumerator.Provider.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.enumerate") DEFAULT_NON_SPLITTABLE_FILE_ENUMERATOR

The default file enumerator used for non-splittable formats. The enumerator recursively enumerates files, creates one split for the file, and filters hidden files (files starting with '.' or '_').

    * ### Method Detail

      * #### getSplitSerializer

            public [SimpleVersionedSerializer](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/core/io/SimpleVersionedSerializer.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.core.io")<[FileSourceSplit](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSourceSplit.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src")> getSplitSerializer()

Description copied from interface: `[Source](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/source/Source.html#getSplitSerializer-->)`

Creates a serializer for the source splits. Splits are serialized when sending them from enumerator to reader, and when checkpointing the reader's current state.

Specified by:
    `[getSplitSerializer](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/source/Source.html#getSplitSerializer-->)` in interface `[Source](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/source/Source.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.source")<[T](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSource.html> "type parameter in FileSource"),[FileSourceSplit](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSourceSplit.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src"),[PendingSplitsCheckpoint](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/PendingSplitsCheckpoint.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src")<[FileSourceSplit](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSourceSplit.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src")>>`
Specified by:
    `[getSplitSerializer](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/AbstractFileSource.html#getSplitSerializer-->)` in class `[AbstractFileSource](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/AbstractFileSource.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src")<[T](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSource.html> "type parameter in FileSource"),[FileSourceSplit](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSourceSplit.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src")>`
Returns:
    The serializer for the split type.
      * #### forRecordStreamFormat

            public static <T> [FileSource.FileSourceBuilder](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSource.FileSourceBuilder.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src")<T> forRecordStreamFormat([StreamFormat](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/reader/StreamFormat.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.reader")<T> reader,
                                                                                    [Path](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/core/fs/Path.html> "class in org.apache.flink.core.fs")... paths)

Builds a new `FileSource` using a [`StreamFormat`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/reader/StreamFormat.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.reader") to read record-by-record from a file stream.

When possible, stream-based formats are generally easier (preferable) to file-based formats, because they support better default behavior around I/O batching or progress tracking (checkpoints).

Stream formats also automatically de-compress files based on the file extension. This supports files ending in ".deflate" (Deflate), ".xz" (XZ), ".bz2" (BZip2), ".gz", ".gzip" (GZip).

      * #### forBulkFileFormat

            public static <T> [FileSource.FileSourceBuilder](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSource.FileSourceBuilder.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src")<T> forBulkFileFormat([BulkFormat](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/reader/BulkFormat.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.reader")<T,[FileSourceSplit](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSourceSplit.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src")> reader,
                                                                                [Path](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/core/fs/Path.html> "class in org.apache.flink.core.fs")... paths)

Builds a new `FileSource` using a [`BulkFormat`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/reader/BulkFormat.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.reader") to read batches of records from files.

Examples for bulk readers are compressed and vectorized formats such as ORC or Parquet.

      * #### forRecordFileFormat

            public static <T> [FileSource.FileSourceBuilder](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/FileSource.FileSourceBuilder.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src")<T> forRecordFileFormat([FileRecordFormat](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/reader/FileRecordFormat.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.reader")<T> reader,
                                                                                  [Path](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/core/fs/Path.html> "class in org.apache.flink.core.fs")... paths)

Builds a new `FileSource` using a [`FileRecordFormat`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/reader/FileRecordFormat.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.reader") to read record-by-record from a a file path.

A `FileRecordFormat` is more general than the [`StreamFormat`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/src/reader/StreamFormat.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.reader"), but also requires often more careful parametrization.
